published_prior_art.cite: Newey1994Econometrica + ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ: same influence function with consistent studentization gives same first-order Wald limit

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1: weak-overlap DML-LRO-ADML studentization frontier, rejected because report matrices were not primitive

published_prior_art.cite: Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR: thresholded DR t-statistics under weak overlap, not a three-report DML/LRO/ADML iff frontier
BrunsSmith2026RieszApproaches: Riesz-estimator equivalence for some regularized classes, not an LRO coefficient-space classification

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal calls the outcome-block selector L_Apub an exact published LRO report, but the verified LRO paper gives a general locally robust GMM/sandwich construction, not this specific ATE outcome-only selector.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: With the locked finite-support q_pub, the propensity-score block is orthogonal to psi_n and to the outcome residual score blocks, so the full ADML projection has zero propensity coefficient and c_AD lies in the outcome-block LRO space; the assumed LRO-ADML separation is vacuous.

soundness_flags.label: Assumption lro-separated
soundness_flags.one_line: The noncontainment premise c_AD notin C_L,Apub contradicts the proposal's own q_pub and L_Apub definitions in the finite-support audit design.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the audited LRO selector or q_pub with a primitive published-report derivation where the ADML coefficient genuinely has a component outside the LRO coefficient space, then recompute Theorem 1.
If the outcome-block selector is retained, pivot the flagship claim to DML versus the common residualized LRO/ADML report, dropping the strict ADML < LRO branch.
Downgrade 'exact published LRO report' to 'audited LRO specialization' unless a specific formula from ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO is cited and matched.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version; the flagship strict LRO-ADML separation is killed by the proposal's own locked finite-support algebra.
